plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-554 RP18.P01.M06.S05-RP18.P02.M02.S22 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-554 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP18 p01 closeout p02 foundation: the P01 Synthetic Fixture Set tail (the sixteen remaining rows), the full P01 Test And Golden Case Set, Hermes Evidence Packet, Claude Review Packet, and Closeout And Next Handoff cycles, and the full P02 Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, and Type And Shape Definition cycles as eight descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H18), Claude review packet (C18), and CP00-555 handoff, without opening AI legal workflows, precedent, clause markup, or DD extraction runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-554 is a descriptor-only RP18 (AI Legal Workflows) closeout/foundation pack covering 150 units RP18.P01.M06.S05-RP18.P02.M02.S22. Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack is well-formed and within its declared boundaries. Coverage: deliverable distribution (implementation:77, ui:23, fixture:4, test:20, hermes_evidence:4, claude_review:7, contract:3, security_audit:6, failure_recovery:6) and micro-title distribution recomputed from the plan's included_units match the prompt baseline, registry.js CP554 requirements, and the contract exactly; units are all RP18 with no duplicates. No-write/no-real-data: service.js builds only frozen descriptor objects with no fs/network/DB/runtime side effects; every no-write attestation flag (writes_product_state, creates/updates_database_rows, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, evaluates_runtime_permission, loads_real_fixture_data) is false; validators, the validator script, and tests only read; no secrets or real client data are present (real_client matches are policy flags asserting false). Authority boundary: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required=true, read_only=true, tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob. Hermes patterns are descriptor-only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), used solely for review packet/receipt naming, not product runtime. Review hardening: readiness auth preflight passed with non-zero valid-JSON stdout; validation-report shows no full diff/source in prompt, no forbidden tools, summary-only prohibited for Risk C, and source_inspection_mode read_tools_used. The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly false (status pending final closeout); production_ready_candidate is a separate descriptor-verification flag and does not assert enterprise trust. Contract is internally consistent: current_pack=CP00-554, all four descriptor sections/requirements/attestations present, historical_pack_bindings terminates at CP00-554, and the validator deepEquals these with reported exit 0. index.js re-exports CP554 functions via wildcard (no change needed); unrelated dirty files (UI workstream, LDIP, workbook) are correctly excluded from pack scope. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-554 ai legal workflows core p01 closeout p02 foundation descriptor pack
